location.office_time_required: up to 10% travel for onboarding, client or partner work, team meetings, and industry events

level_signals: 12+ years of experience in Product Management
Leads multiple product areas and cross-functional alignment without direct authority
Base Pay Range $196,000–$294,000 USD consistent with Staff/Principal-level IC

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 12+ years of experience in Product Management with significant exposure to Enterprise SaaS products.
Proven success leading enterprise-grade or platform products such as CRM, marketing automation, customer data platforms, analytics, data infrastructure, or AI-driven systems.
Direct experience supporting large enterprise customers (e.g., Fortune 1000), not just SMB or self-serve products.
Strong intuition for what drives enterprise purchasing decisions: trust, compliance, scalability, configurability, integrations, and roadmap credibility.
Hands-on experience partnering with Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Customer Success on enterprise deals.
Comfortable discussing system architecture, data models, APIs, integrations, data flows, and security standards with engineering teams.
Experience working on products with complex data infrastructure, distributed systems, or significant integration dependencies.
Proven ability to influence and lead across Product, Engineering, GTM, Security, and Legal without direct authority.
Track record of translating enterprise customer needs into clear product requirements, thoughtful tradeoffs, and scalable platform solutions.
Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to align diverse teams around shared priorities.

You've already experimented with AI in work or personal projects, and you're excited to dive in and learn fast. You're hungry to responsibly explore new AI tools and workflows, finding ways to make your work smarter and more efficient.

scope.surface: Enterprise platform product areas including unified customer data, AI-driven automation, and ecosystem integrations; B2C CRM for Enterprise
scope.business_model: B2B SaaS, enterprise-focused expansion, ARR growth
primary_metrics_inferred: OKRs tied to enterprise platform scalability and governance
core_responsibilities: Set and evangelize a 2–3 year vision for Klaviyo's Enterprise platform
Translate company goals into clear OKRs and roadmaps that accelerate Enterprise adoption, retention, and ARR growth
Represent the voice of large, global customers in strategic product discussions
Deliver capabilities that unify customer data across channels, enable real-time personalization, and support global data governance and compliance
Collaborate deeply with Engineering, Design, Sales, Solutions, Customer Success, Security, and Legal
Lead roadmap reviews, facilitate strategic tradeoffs, and ensure alignment across teams
Act as a central point of coordination for enterprise commitments from RFP support and security reviews through onboarding and long-term success
Transform workflows by putting AI at the center, building smarter systems and ways of working from the ground up

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as part of total compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed and wide ($196K–$294K base), reflecting US geographic flexibility
Explicit encouragement to apply even for close-but-not-exact matches
Clear product surface and multi-area ownership described in detail
High-visibility, strategic role at center of key company growth initiative
Company has 183,000+ brand customers — real scale to work with
AI fluency encouraged with responsible use framing
red_flags: 12+ years minimum is a very high bar that may exclude strong mid-career PMs
Role owns 'multiple product areas' simultaneously — potential scope creep or resourcing risk
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not stated — unclear for international applicants
Up to 10% travel required — may not be ideal for fully location-fixed candidates
Covey AEDT usage for NYC applicants flagged — algorithmic screening may reduce visibility

location.type (inferred as remote-us from salary range covering 'various U.S. geographic markets' and no city requirement stated in body)

ambiguities: Location type is ambiguous: no specific city is named for the role, and the JD references a broad US salary range by geography, suggesting remote-us eligibility, but no explicit 'remote' label is used in the JD body.

The title 'Senior Lead Product Manager' does not map cleanly to standard IC levels (Senior, Staff, Principal) — treated as Staff/Principal-level equivalent given comp and scope.
